p.hi: Bark Cape Horn Pigeon—Owners’ outward account, 1890, Inward account, and interest account to January 30, 1890, with J. and W. R. Wing.

row.cell: By freight collected ship Syren, paid on two boats sent to California, and ship condemned at Rio Janeiro
Sale old metal, sail, boat boards
By 1 box whale craft on hand at California, transferred to new account
By sale lances to Wright, Bame & Co
By balance to debit owner account, this date
To 2 boats left at San Francisco from last outward account
To paid goods sent to San Francisco; Wm. Bayliss, provisions sent California
Labor, $2; E. B. and F. Macey, whalecraft, $34.36
R. Allan, casks sent to California.
J. and W. R. Wing & Co., clothing, etc
T. M. Hart & Co., duck and twine
Share expenses New York account freight
Pemmican meat, $19.87; N. B. Cordage Co., $291.34.
E. Peirce, bomb lances and guns.
E. S. Taber, blocks sent California
Ship B. F. Packard, freight goods to California
Paid ship Syren, special averages on 2 boats sent in Syren, and vessel condemned at Rio Janeiro.
Freight and expenses on boat, originally shipped by ship Lycen
Ship Jas. Drummond, freight goods California
Paid telegram account, officer, 63 cents; bomb lances, $2
Capt. T. Scullen, passage to Chicago
Telegrams account, captain and merchandise account
Paid extra passage Captain Scullen to Chicago
Cartage goods at California, sent in spring, 1889
W. H. Crook, ship carpenter, services
B. F. Wing, services fitting vessel
To use horse and carriage for business
Haslett & Bailey, storage goods at California
Paid Weed & Kingwell, copper nails, etc
Share W. R. Wing, traveling expenses to and from California, and while there on business.
Paid 4,485 gallons oil, shooks, at 2¼ cents
Paid labor, $27.21; W. Lewis, guns and lances, $69
1 pair opera glasses sent by Captain Scullen
Ticket, Captain Scullen, home and return
S. H. Frank & Co., carting casks
Rigging leather, $2.25; M. E. Mayhew, medicines, $23.10
G. M. Josselyn & Co., spikes, etc
Telegram, $1.80; C. L. Le Gal, blocks, etc., $2.75.
Denham, Carrigan & Co., spikes and chandlery
Cooper labor, $3; L. Ford, sail, etc., $19.60.
Cooper bolt and turpentine, $2; medicine, captain, $0.50
Hooks and thimbles, 50 cents; S. E. Slade, lumber, $7.70
Paid S. E. Slade & Co., lumber
Labor, $96.25; J. W. Ganin & Co., hose, $12.50.
E. H. Bucknam & Co., hose pipe, leads, etc.
G. M. Josselyn & Co., bar copper and plugs
Socks for natives, 75 cents; carting dirt, $1.15.
Roth, Blum & Co., beef and pork
Roth, Blum & Co., pigs and provisions
A. Crawford & Co., boat compass, etc
Bread, tea, and coffee, 35 cents; expense, captain, trunk, 50 cents
F. Gregg, sawdust, $2.50; Tubbs & Co., cordage, $65.31
S. S. Arnheim & Co., glass, etc
Eureka Restaurant, board, 5 Kanakas came and went in ship
Paid L. Levy, tablecloths and boots
Telegram, $1; S. E. Slade & Co., lumber, etc., $12.95
A. H. Keys, meat, $9.24; Goodall & Perkins, water, $70.55
Dillon & Co., repairing chronometer and barometer
T. P. H. Whitelaw, cordage, spikes, etc
Paid Wright, Bame & Co., chandlery, cordage, etc
Captain Scullun, board, $5; S. Sachman, liquors, $16.45
Paid advances additional by W. R. Wing
N. B. Copper Company, yellow metal on bottom
